name: Hive

runner.notes: Install Hive, prepare a run worktree, open it in Hive's local browser UI, provide prompt.md exactly, then run harness verification.

inclusion: Direct ADE competitor centered on local browser-based orchestration of CLI agent teams.
pricing_model: Source-available/local install; bring-your-own agent CLIs and subscriptions.
public_stack_signals: Official site says npm global install, local 127.0.0.1 browser app, Node >= 22, BSL 1.1.
Runs Claude Code, Codex, Gemini and other real CLI processes in browser terminals.
Task graph is .hive/tasks.md in the repository.
moat_hypotheses: PWA/mobile remote access while execution stays local
setup_notes: Install with npm i -g @tt-a1i/hive and open the printed 127.0.0.1 URL.

sources.notes: Product model, CLI agent list, install, Node requirement, local/remote claims.
Linked official source repository from product page. Refresh 2026-07-15: ~420 stars; last push 2026-06-18 (slower than Orca/Kandev/Fusion); BSL remains via LICENSE.BSL path for post-0.6.0-alpha.8.

research.positioning: A local browser workbench for an orchestrator to dispatch tasks to CLI agent workers.

unknowns: Internal database/storage implementation not mapped in this pass.

infrastructure: Optional remote access pairs a phone/browser with the local machine; execution stays local.
No account/key required for Hive itself according to official site.

orchestration: Orchestrator dispatches workers via team send
Workers report back via team report
review_shipping: Write/review and test/fix patterns are central examples; final diff remains user-reviewed
remote_mobile: Optional remote access with E2E encryption
Phone/browser can type, dispatch, and steer after pairing
No public ports required; local machine dials outbound

parity_risks: Team protocol may need a benchmark prompt variant that asks orchestrator to delegate.
Remote phone access is out of scope for current local task suite.

hypotheses: Repo-native task graph could make agent teamwork auditable and portable.
Browser-local install lowers desktop app friction.
Explicit team send/report model is easy to observe in benchmarks.
evidence: Official site documents .hive/tasks.md, team memory, auto-staff, workflows, and local 127.0.0.1 execution.
Official site documents optional E2E remote access with execution staying local.
unknowns: How robustly Hive preserves worktrees and review evidence under dirty-worktree tasks remains untested.
